other_observers: Andrew Spencer, Peter Gent, John Drummond for the second flyover, others I cannot recall; Do not recall
duration: 6 to 8 seconds first look and 1 to 2 sec second look
specific_location: MacKenzie Ave Bridge over the Arkansas River
description: Bird flew up to an exposed perch on the south side of the river, about 80 meters from the bridge. Bird was strongly backlit, but large yellow cere and completely black plumage were still visible. Black eye. The bird was there for about 6 to 8 seconds; got scope views. The bird 'lost its balance' for a second, flapping its wings a bit and flashing us a beautiful view of its tail, with a wide white median band, a black subterminal band of roughly equal width, and a very thin white terminal band. Then the bird took off and flew through the trees towards us, crossing the road directly south of us a few moments later. Got brief 1-2 second binocular views of it in flight as it crossed the road in good light. Completely blackish upperparts/upperwings and large white tail band visible again in flight. Tail quite short for a buteo.Behaviors: See above.Call: bird was silentPlumage: adult
